name: profile
title: Profile
help: Capture profile: a sensible parameter bundle applied for options you did not set explicitly (your explicit choices always win). 'drone-3d': aerial survey (spatial matching, balanced resolution). 'object': finds/artefacts/turntable (exhaustive matching, full-resolution densify, finest refine, local frame). 'architecture': buildings/facades (exhaustive matching, balanced resolution). 'none': defaults only. Default: %(default)s

name: sparse-engine
title: Sparse Engine
help: Structure-from-Motion backend for the sparse reconstruction. 'colmap' is recommended for close-range / convergent imagery (objects, architecture); 'opensfm' for GPS-tagged aerial sets. Default: %(default)s

name: matcher
title: COLMAP Matcher
help: Feature matching strategy (COLMAP only). 'exhaustive' is most robust for unordered object photos but scales O(n^2). 'sequential' for ordered/video frames. 'vocab_tree' scales to large sets (hundreds–thousands of images): each image retrieves its most similar images from a baked-in Flickr100K vocabulary tree instead of matching all pairs. 'spatial' uses GPS when present. Default: %(default)s

name: mapper
title: COLMAP Mapper
help: Sparse SfM strategy (COLMAP only). 'incremental' is the robust default for close-range / convergent sets (objects, architecture). 'global' uses COLMAP 4's built-in GLOMAP global mapper — much faster on large, well-connected (e.g. aerial) blocks, but less robust on convergent close-range imagery; prefer it for big sets / ODM-comparison runs, not as the default. Default: %(default)s

name: camera-model
title: Camera Model
help: COLMAP camera model used for self-calibration. FULL_OPENCV for high-distortion / macro lenses. Default: %(default)s

name: densify-resolution-level
title: Densify Resolution Level
help: OpenMVS DensifyPointCloud downscale level. 0 = full resolution (slowest, densest). Default: %(default)s

name: number-views-fuse
title: Number Views Fuse
help: Minimum number of views that must agree to fuse a 3D point. Higher = cleaner but sparser. ODM uses 2; 3-4 improves close-range quality. Default: %(default)s

name: skip-reconstruct-mesh
title: Skip Mesh Reconstruction
help: Skip OpenMVS ReconstructMesh/RefineMesh/TextureMesh and stop after the dense point cloud. The full mesh chain is the point of this engine and runs by default; check only when you need a cloud-only fast run. Default: %(default)s

name: refine-mesh-iters
title: Refine Mesh Iterations
help: OpenMVS RefineMesh multi-scale refinement iterations (passed to RefineMesh --scales: photometric mesh optimisation against the source images, repeated over image scales). 0 disables RefineMesh entirely. This is the main quality lever vs. commercial tools. Default: %(default)s

name: refine-max-face-area
title: Refine: Max Face Area (px)
help: RefineMesh subdivision threshold: maximum projected face area in pixels before a triangle is split. Smaller = finer mesh and more detail recovered, at higher cost (try 8 for maximum detail, 32 for speed). Default: %(default)s

name: refine-gradient-step
title: Refine: Gradient Step
help: RefineMesh gradient descent step size. Larger = more aggressive surface movement per iteration (risk of artefacts), smaller = conservative refinement. Default: %(default)s

name: cpu-threads
title: CPU Threads (SIFT/Match)
help: Worker threads for COLMAP CPU feature extraction/matching. Capped low by default because the CPU SIFT extractor spikes RAM per thread (OOM risk on large images); raise on hosts with plenty of RAM. Ignored on GPU. Default: %(default)s

name: cpu-match-block
title: CPU Matcher Block Size
help: COLMAP exhaustive-matcher block size on the CPU path. The default FLANN matcher segfaults at large blocks on some hosts (arm64); 10 is safe. Raise cautiously for speed on x86. Ignored on GPU. Default: %(default)s

name: free-space-support
title: Mesh: Free-Space Support
help: OpenMVS ReconstructMesh --free-space-support: use visibility rays to recover weakly-supported surfaces (building walls seen only at grazing angles in nadir flights, textureless areas). Off by OpenMVS default; enable for facades. Default: %(default)s

name: mesh-close-holes
title: Mesh: Close Holes (faces)
help: OpenMVS ReconstructMesh --close-holes: maximum hole size (in faces) to close in the reconstructed mesh. The default 30 closes only small gaps; raise (e.g. 300) to bridge missing facades Metashape-style — bridged areas carry interpolated geometry, not real reconstruction. Default: %(default)s

name: mesh-decimate
title: Mesh Decimation Factor
help: ReconstructMesh decimation. 1.0 = no decimation (full detail). 0.5 = halve faces. Default: %(default)s

name: texture-resolution
title: Texture Resolution
help: TextureMesh output texture atlas size in px. Default: %(default)s

name: skip-color-harmonize
title: Skip Texture Color Harmonization
help: Skip the per-image exposure/colour equalisation before texturing. By default one RGB gain per image (estimated from the sparse-point observations) is applied to the undistorted images, so the texture atlas and orthophoto are assembled from photometrically consistent photos — it replaces OpenMVS seam leveling, which is corrupted on this build. Default: %(default)s

name: skip-view-blending
title: Skip Multi-View Texture Blending
help: Skip the multi-view texture blend: by default every texel is re-baked as a weighted blend of its best source views (view angle and distance weighted, occlusion-tested against mesh depth maps) instead of OpenMVS's single best view — removes per-view exposure/sharpness blotches on homogeneous surfaces (roofs). Default: %(default)s

name: skip-seam-smoothing
title: Skip Texture Seam Smoothing
help: Skip Effigies' own seam leveling: by default, colours are equalised across texture-patch seams (sampled inset from each side, screened-Poisson diffusion into the patch interiors, baked into the atlas) — replaces OpenMVS's corrupted seam leveling. Default: %(default)s

name: texture-seam-leveling
title: Texture Seam Leveling
help: OpenMVS global+local seam leveling (colour harmonisation between views). OFF by default: it corrupts texture patches on this (arm64/CPU) build — black patches with saturated borders — verified broken in BOTH v2.4.0 and master (2026-06). Use the default exposure harmonisation instead (skip-color-harmonize to disable). Default: %(default)s

name: georeference
title: Georeferencing
help: Georeferencing strategy. 'auto': use gcp-file if present, else EXIF-GPS, else fall back to a metrically-scaled local model. 'gcp': require a GCP file. 'exif': require EXIF-GPS. 'none': skip georeferencing entirely and keep a local (object-centric) frame - recommended for turntable / close-range objects. Default: %(default)s

name: crs
title: Target CRS (EPSG)
help: Target projected CRS for georeferencing, given as an EPSG code (e.g. 'EPSG:32633'). 'auto' derives the appropriate UTM zone automatically from the GPS/GCP coordinates. Any projected EPSG code is accepted, including regional grids. Default: %(default)s

name: gcp
title: GCP File
help: Optional path to a ground control points file (ODM gcp_list.txt format) used for the Helmert georeferencing bridge. Default: none

name: skip-orthophoto
title: Skip Orthophoto
help: Skip generating the orthophoto GeoTIFF. By default a georeferenced orthophoto is nadir-rasterised from the refined textured mesh (odm_orthophoto/odm_orthophoto.tif); it is skipped automatically for non-georeferenced (local-frame) results. Default: %(default)s

name: orthophoto-resolution
title: Orthophoto Resolution (cm/px)
help: Orthophoto ground sample distance in cm/pixel. 'auto' targets ~4k px across the scene (clamped 1 cm .. 1 m). Smaller = sharper but larger file. Default: %(default)s

name: no-gpu
title: No GPU
help: Force CPU processing even when a CUDA GPU is available. Without this flag the engine uses the GPU when present and falls back to CPU automatically (with a warning) when not. Default: %(default)s
